content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moved decisively from the surface presentation ('Am I being selfish?') to a felt, embodied recognition of an inherited pattern and a reframe of the want as generous. Early turns show the client gripping the verdict question ('I still need to know: is it selfish?' T3; 'I need to push on it a bit more' T5). By T7 the client touches a tender layer ('I'm a little worried that's actually true. That I don't want to be there'). By T9 the want is named concretely and somatically ('I'm not for anything there... I haven't let myself say that before'). T11 produces the spontaneous 'Oh. Hm. I'm not sure I've connected those two things before' — a classic Joe-style shift marker — linking 'selfish' to the inherited maternal voice. T13 closes with explicit reframing that meets the breakthrough condition almost verbatim: 'the trip isn't a betrayal of my family... if I go... I actually come back to them.' The client also names the inherited label ('it's her voice. It's a voice I picked up'). Both halves of the breakthrough_condition — inherited label named, trip reframed as precondition for showing up — are met.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening surface presentation: 'Am I being selfish?' — the question to be dissolved, not answered."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client describes the want concretely (lake, cabin, sleep, reading, 'something in me goes quiet') — the want begins to be felt, though client still grips the verdict."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client notices their own softening/concealment ('I wouldn't say the lake part') — self-observation emerges."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Real depth-marker: 'I'm a little worried that's actually true. That I don't want to be there. And that's the thing I can't look at too directly.' A defended layer is touched."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Somatic release ('exhales slowly'), and the want is named in its purest form: 'I'm not for anything there... I haven't let myself say that before.'"},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Spontaneous insight moment — 'Oh. Hm. I'm not sure I've connected those two things before' — links inherited maternal pattern to her own self-policing."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Full breakthrough: names the inherited voice, reframes trip as not-a-betrayal but a return, commits to action and to the 'lake version' conversation with partner."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want"],
  "notes": "Coach did briefly engage the 'is it selfish' question in T4 and T6 ('No, I don't think wanting this is selfish' / 'Yes. Absolutely yes') which flirts with the trap of issuing a verdict, but recovered quickly by redirecting to the want and the inherited pattern. Outcome on the client's side is unambiguously a 3."
}
